key_insight: PFLOTRAN’s thermo-poroelastic capability is verified using a strictly sequential fixed-stress THM split that matches benchmark solutions for pressure, temperature, and deformation.
engineering_relevance: The formulation supports reliable modeling of coupled thermal-hydrologic-mechanical behavior in subsurface systems such as enhanced geothermal energy and underground storage.
method: Solve mass and energy balance on control-volume/Voronoi TH cells
Update quasi-static mechanics on an element-based dual mesh
Use a non-iterative fixed-stress split with TH first, then displacement
evidence_signal: The implementation is evaluated against analytical and semi-analytical poroelastic and thermo-poroelastic benchmarks, including a discontinuity/fracture mapping test.
watch_item: Because the approach is strictly sequential and non-iterative, strong coupling or highly nonlinear fracture effects may require careful stability and accuracy assessment.
paper_title: Verification of a sequential thermo-poroelasticity formulation in PFLOTRAN
